text: Within ~5 years, probably ~5 times as many satellites as rest of world https://t.co/EODE2lcbM0
summary: Within ~5 years, probably ~5 times as many satellites as rest of world
ai_summary: Elon Musk projects that SpaceX will deploy approximately 5 times more satellites than all other companies combined within 5 years, underscoring Starlink's dominant market position in the satellite internet space. This statement reflects SpaceX's aggressive scaling strategy and competitive moat in low-earth orbit satellite deployment, demonstrating how concentrated technological advantage and manufacturing capability can create market leadership in emerging infrastructure sectors.
embed_html:
Within ~5 years, probably ~5 times as many satellites as rest of world https://t.co/EODE2lcbM0

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) June 13, 2026

text: @PalmerLuckey @paranoidream That is what they told me

parent.text: "There are only 25 satellites launched a year, every year, and that's not going to change." Ryhmes with the current defense ecosystem.
parent.name: Palmer Luckey
parent.embed_html:
"There are only 25 satellites launched a year, every year, and that's not going to change."

Ryhmes with the current defense ecosystem. https://t.co/PfH7cha3Ew

— Palmer Luckey (@PalmerLuckey) June 13, 2026

ai_summary: Palmer Luckey highlights a constraint in the defense/satellite industry where only ~25 satellites launch annually, suggesting systemic limitations in the current ecosystem. Elon's reply confirms he's encountered this same skepticism about satellite launch capacity, likely referencing SpaceX's efforts to dramatically increase launch cadence and Starlink's need for thousands of satellites. This illustrates a key business challenge: overcoming entrenched industry assumptions and bottlenecks that incumbents accept as immutable constraints.
embed_html:
That is what they told me

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) June 13, 2026
